module: Sepsis and Septic Shock Part IV: Adjuvant therapies in sepsis

questions.question: Which of the following statements is true regarding the use of Intravenous Immunoglobulin (IVIG) in sepsis?
options: A: The SSC gives a strong recommendation for the use of IVIG in refractory septic shock
B: IVIG has been shown effective in immunomodulating the excessive inflammatory response in early sepsis
C: IVIG containing higher fractions of IgM have been proven to have significant effects on sepsis mortality in a large RCT
D: No studies have been completed on the use of IVIG as an adjunct treatment for patients with sepsis
E: The current recommendation of the SSC in the 2016 Guidelines is against the use of IVIG (Consider omitting)
questions.explanation: The current SSC recommendation is against the use of IV immunoglobulins in patients with sepsis (weak recommendation). The rationale for IVIG is augmentation of the immune response to infection (not immunomodulatory/down-regulatory). Studies including those with higher IgM fractions have been conflicting. The SSC does not make a strong recommendation for IVIG in any sepsis subgroup.
questions.topic: IVIG SSC recommendation against immunoglobulin sepsis

questions.question: A 70 kg male is being managed in the ICU for Septic shock. He was resuscitated with fluids, empiric antibiotics and is no more fluid responsive as assessed by passive leg raise. He is currently on 20 mcg/min of Norepinephrine to maintain MAP >65 mmHg. Despite 6 hours into the treatment, he still requires escalating doses of Norepinephrine. What is the next best step in the management of this patient?
options: A: Add Phenylephrine as a second vasopressor agent
B: Give 25% hyper oncotic albumin infusion
C: Check random cortisol and if low, add high dose dexamethasone
D: Test for ACTH stimulation test (Cosyntropin/Synacthen) to check for adrenal reserve
E: Add Hydrocortisone 50 mg Q6h IV
questions.explanation: Current recommendations for corticosteroids include: when NE or Epi >0.25 mcg/kg/min for >4 hours, administer IV Hydrocortisone <400 mg/day in divided doses (e.g. 50 mg Q6h = 200 mg/day). Phenylephrine is a weak alpha agonist and adds little when NE is already on board. Patient is not fluid responsive so albumin has no benefit. There is no need to check random cortisol or perform ACTH testing before steroid administration in vasopressor-dependent septic shock — cortisol levels vary significantly in critically ill patients and ACTH testing does not predict response. High-dose dexamethasone is also to be avoided.
questions.topic: hydrocortisone septic shock CIRCI no ACTH test vasopressor threshold

questions.question: Which of the following statements is true in regard to the use of corticosteroids in septic shock?
options: A: If given early in the course of sepsis, corticosteroids have been proven to prevent the progression of sepsis to septic shock
B: In septic patients responsive to fluids but with major trauma corticosteroids are indicated
C: The recommended drug and dose of corticosteroid for vasopressor unresponsive septic shock is IV hydrocortisone <400 mg/day
D: ACTH testing should be used to determine the appropriateness of corticosteroid administration
E: Corticosteroids should be used whenever the norepinephrine dose requirement exceeds 0.2 µg/kg/min
questions.explanation: Hydrocortisone <400 mg/day (typically 200 or 300 mg/day) for ≥3 days can be considered for patients with septic shock not responsive to fluid and moderate-to-high-dose vasopressor therapy. Corticosteroids are NOT suggested for patients with major trauma (conditional, low quality of evidence). ACTH testing is not used to determine appropriateness — cortisol levels and ACTH responses are unreliable in critically ill patients. Corticosteroids do not prevent progression from sepsis to septic shock.
questions.topic: hydrocortisone <400mg corticosteroids septic shock ACTH testing trauma

questions.question: Which of the following therapies is presently recommended by the Surviving Sepsis Campaign Guidelines as an adjunct for the treatment of septic shock?
options: A: Hemoadsorption with polymyxin B-immobilized polystyrene derived fibers
B: Angiotensin II
C: Erythropoietin
D: IV hydrocortisone
E: Low-dose dopamine (for renal protection)
questions.explanation: Of the potential adjuncts listed, only IV hydrocortisone was given a positive recommendation by the SSC. The recommendation is: if adequate fluid resuscitation and vasopressor therapy are unable to restore haemodynamic stability, suggest IV hydrocortisone at a dose of 200 mg/day (weak recommendation, low quality of evidence). Angiotensin II was not specifically addressed in the most recent SSC guidelines. Low-dose dopamine for renal protection is specifically recommended against (strong recommendation, high quality). Erythropoietin is recommended against (strong recommendation). No recommendation is made regarding hemoadsorption/blood purification techniques.
questions.topic: SSC adjunct hydrocortisone dopamine erythropoietin hemoadsorption angiotensin

questions.question: According to the Transfusion Requirements In Septic Shock (TRISS) trial, which of the following statements is true?
options: A: A liberal hemoglobin transfusion threshold of 9 g/dL resulted in a lower rate of ischemic events than a restrictive threshold of 7 g/dL
B: The transfusion of red blood cell units of less than 7 days storage time resulted in a lower mortality rate than red blood cell units of greater than 21 days storage time
C: In the setting of myocardial ischemia, red blood cell transfusion should be restricted to when haemoglobin levels are <6 g/dL
D: A restrictive transfusion threshold of 7 g/dL resulted in a similar 90-day mortality rate as a liberal threshold of 9 g/dL
E: In the setting of active hemorrhage, a transfusion threshold of 10 g/dL is recommended
questions.explanation: The TRISS trial found that a restrictive transfusion threshold of 7 g/dL resulted in similar rates of ischemic events, usage of life support, and 90-day mortality compared to a liberal threshold of 9 g/dL, with the lower-threshold group receiving significantly fewer transfusions. SSC guidelines therefore recommend against RBC transfusion for Hb >7 g/dL in the absence of myocardial ischaemia, severe hypoxaemia, or acute haemorrhage. The TRISS trial was not designed to study effects of RBC storage age. In myocardial ischaemia, no Hb threshold is presently defined but transfusion for levels above 7 g/dL is considered appropriate in that specific setting.
questions.topic: TRISS trial transfusion threshold 7 g/dL restrictive liberal 90-day mortality

questions.question: Regarding serum values, compared with normal subjects, critically ill patients have: (POMC: pro-opio melanocortin)
options: A: Low cortisol, low ACTH, low POMC
B: High cortisol, low ACTH, high POMC
C: High cortisol, high ACTH, high POMC
D: Low cortisol, high ACTH, low POMC
E: Low cortisol, high ACTH, high POMC
questions.explanation: Critical illness is characterised by high cortisol levels (due to decreased clearance), which causes negative feedback inhibition on ACTH production. The step that cortisol inhibits is the conversion of POMC to ACTH, thus increasing serum POMC and decreasing ACTH levels. This increased cortisol is not ACTH-driven but due to decreased clearance — referred to as 'ACTH-cortisol dissociation', persisting for at least 3 weeks of ICU admission. Week 1: High Cortisol, Low ACTH. Weeks 1-3: High to Normal Cortisol, Low to Normal ACTH. >Week 3: Normal Cortisol, Normal to High ACTH.
questions.topic: CIRCI cortisol ACTH POMC dissociation critically ill decreased clearance

questions.question: Choose true or false for each of the following statements about the mechanisms of Critical Illness Related Corticosteroid Insufficiency (CIRCI).

statements.label: A
statements.text: Activation of ACTH synthesis due to positive feedback for insufficient cortisol

statements.label: B
statements.text: Impaired cortisol clearance due to downregulated production of cortisol binding globulin

statements.label: C
statements.text: Enhanced plasma clearance of cortisol due to increased 5-alpha reductase activity

statements.label: D
statements.text: Decreased Glucocorticoid Receptor (GR) activity causing target tissue resistance despite high cortisol levels

questions.explanation: In septic shock, ACTH production is INHIBITED (not stimulated) due to negative feedback by circulating cortisol (A=False). There is increased volume of distribution of cortisol due to impaired binding to cortisol binding globulin (CBG) — CBG and albumin are negative acute phase reactants, their downregulated production reduces cortisol-CBG binding (B=True). 5-alpha reductase activity is DECREASED — this DECREASES plasma clearance of cortisol (C=False). Despite high cortisol levels, there is target tissue resistance due to decreased glucocorticoid receptor (GR) activity (D=True).
questions.topic: CIRCI mechanisms ACTH CBG 5-alpha reductase GR resistance cortisol

questions.question: Sepsis is characterised by endotoxemia and circulating cytokines. One theoretical strategy is to clear the circulation of endotoxins and cytokines using either a high cut off membrane or increasing the dose of CRRT. Based on the best available evidence, application of high intensity dose (>35ml/kg/hour) compared to less intense dose (20ml/kg/hour) has demonstrated to:

statements.label: A
statements.text: Decrease mortality in high intensity group

statements.label: B
statements.text: Increase electrolyte abnormalities

statements.label: C
statements.text: No difference in vasopressor use

statements.label: D
statements.text: Decreased urine output with delayed renal recovery

questions.explanation: High-intensity CRRT dose does NOT improve outcomes including mortality (A=False); rather it can result in electrolyte abnormalities such as hypokalaemia and hypophosphataemia (B=True), an INCREASE in vasopressor use (C=False — there IS a difference, high intensity increases vasopressor use), and decreased urine output with delayed renal recovery (D=True). There may also be increased incidence of osmotic disequilibrium syndrome with very high doses such as 40 ml/kg/hr. The mortality benefit seen in the RENAL trial was related to negative ultrafiltration rate, not dose.
questions.topic: CRRT high intensity dose mortality electrolyte vasopressor renal recovery

questions.question: Which of the following adjunctive therapies for sepsis is presently recommended based upon available clinical evidence?
options: A: Intravenous immunoglobulin in patients with vasopressors refractory septic shock
B: Deep vein thrombosis prophylaxis with heparins (unfractionated or low-molecular weight, in the absence of haemorrhage)
C: Intensive glucose control, with blood glucose targets of 90-120 mg/dL (5-6.7 mmol/L)
D: Erythropoietin administration for sepsis-associated anaemia with haemoglobin levels <7 g/dL
E: Platelet transfusions should be given when the platelet count is below 50,000/mm³
questions.explanation: DVT prophylaxis with heparins (LMWH preferably, or UFH) is strongly recommended for septic patients in the absence of contraindications. Sepsis is a risk factor for VTE especially with hypotension, shock, and vasopressor use. SSC 2021 suggests against IVIG (weak recommendation). Intensive glucose control (90-120 mg/dL) is harmful (NICE-SUGAR) — target is ≤180 mg/dL. The SSC issued a strong recommendation against erythropoietin. The SSC 2016 guidelines suggest prophylactic platelets at <10,000/mm³ (absent bleeding) or <20,000/mm³ with significant haemorrhage risk; most recent guidelines make no recommendations regarding platelet transfusions.
questions.topic: DVT prophylaxis heparin LMWH sepsis recommended adjunctive therapy

questions.question: The following is a true statement regarding the use of Ilofotase alfa in sepsis:
options: A: Mechanism of action is by dephosphorylating ATP
B: Mechanism of action by enhancing the metabolism of TNF alpha
C: It is the drug of choice for toxic shock syndrome caused by gram positive bacteria
D: It is the drug of choice for disseminated intravascular coagulation related to Sepsis
E: None of the above is correct
questions.explanation: Ilofotase alfa is exogenous recombinant alkaline phosphatase which was designed to detoxify PAMPs (e.g., endotoxin) and de-phosphorylates DAMPs (ATP — option A is correct mechanism). Adenosine released after ATP dephosphorylation activates immunosuppressive pathways via A2 alpha receptors, reducing release of pro-inflammatory mediators including TNF-alpha (option B describes a secondary mechanism, not the primary action). However, none of options A-D as written is fully correct: A says 'dephosphorylating ATP' which is actually correct — but the question asks for the TRUE statement, and ESICM's answer is 'none of the above'. Ilofotase alfa is NOT the drug of choice for TSS (option C) or DIC (option D). In the STOP-AKI trial, it failed to show benefit on creatinine clearance but improved long-term renal function.
questions.topic: ilofotase alfa alkaline phosphatase dephosphorylates ATP DAMPs PAMPs mechanism

questions.question: Erythropoietin administration is not recommended for the treatment of anemia in patients with sepsis. Based on current evidence, choose the correct statement about the outcomes of erythropoietin administration in critically ill patients.
options: A: Less RBC transfusion, high mortality, high thrombotic episodes
B: Less RBC transfusion, no effect on mortality, high thrombotic episodes
C: No effect of RBC transfusion, high mortality, high thrombotic episodes
D: No effect of RBC transfusion or mortality, thrombotic episodes
E: No effect of RBC transfusion or mortality, high thrombotic episodes
questions.explanation: EPO administration causes only a modest increase in Hb with slightly reduced need for blood transfusion (less RBC transfusions). However, this modest response may be secondary to inflammatory mediator resistance to EPO, requiring very high doses. There is no significant effect on overall mortality, length of stay in hospital or ICU. The only justification for its use is specific medical indications such as renal disease or patients refusing allogeneic transfusions (Jehovah's Witnesses). Importantly, EPO increases thrombotic episodes.
questions.topic: erythropoietin EPO sepsis anemia less RBC transfusion no mortality high thrombosis

questions.question: With regards to stress ulceration prophylaxis in the critically ill patient, which of the following are correct?

statements.label: A
statements.text: There is strong evidence that stress ulcer prophylaxis reduces mortality in septic patients

statements.label: B
statements.text: Histamine H2 receptor antagonists (H2RAs) are superior to proton-pump inhibitors in preventing gastrointestinal ulceration in critically ill patients

statements.label: C
statements.text: Sucralfate is not as effective as H2RAs in preventing stress ulceration

statements.label: D
statements.text: Evidence suggests that higher rates of hospital-acquired and aspiration pneumonia are seen in patients taking acid-suppressive medication

questions.explanation: There is no strong evidence that stress ulcer prophylaxis reduces mortality (A=False). The SSC guidelines recommend using either PPIs or H2RAs when stress ulcer prophylaxis is indicated — they are equivalent, H2RAs are NOT superior to PPIs (B=False). Sucralfate IS less effective than H2RAs in preventing stress ulceration (C=True). Acid-suppressive medication (both PPIs and H2RAs) is associated with higher rates of hospital-acquired and aspiration pneumonia (D=True).
questions.topic: stress ulcer prophylaxis PPI H2RA sucralfate mortality aspiration pneumonia

questions.question: Blood purification techniques have recently gained interest for the management of septic patients, with the idea of removing endotoxin (100kDa) or cytokines (TNF ~54kDa). As per our current understanding, please choose true or false for the following statements regarding various blood purification techniques:

statements.label: A
statements.text: Polymyxin B hemoperfusion directly targets cytokine removal and has been shown to improve outcomes in patients with cytokine storm syndrome related to Sepsis

statements.label: B
statements.text: Oxiris targets both cytokines and endotoxins and when used with CRRT in Septic patients with AKI is shown to have beneficial hemodynamic effect

statements.label: C
statements.text: Cytosorb clears both proinflammatory and anti-inflammatory cytokines

statements.label: D
statements.text: Cytosorb clears Meropenem and the efficacy may be reduced

questions.explanation: Polymyxin B hemoperfusion targets ENDOTOXIN (not cytokines directly) — it is an antibiotic immobilized to polystyrene fibers for endotoxin removal. The cytokine level decrease seen is secondary to endotoxin removal (A=False). Oxiris when used with CRRT in septic patients with endotoxin levels >0.03 EU/ml and AKI has shown decreased cytokines and reduction in norepinephrine dose — beneficial hemodynamic effect (B=True). Cytosorb clears both pro- and anti-inflammatory cytokines — as per peak concentration hypothesis (C=True). Cytosorb does NOT clinically significantly clear Meropenem; it clears Vancomycin, Tobramycin, Fluconazole, Linezolid, and liposomal Amphotericin B. Net influence on beta-lactam pharmacokinetics appears minimal (D=False).
questions.topic: blood purification polymyxin B hemoperfusion Oxiris Cytosorb endotoxin cytokines

questions.question: Which of the following anticoagulants is presently recommended for use in the septic patient?
options: A: Recombinant Activated Protein C
B: Prophylactic Low-molecular weight heparin
C: IV unfractionated heparin
D: Thrombomodulin
E: Antithrombin
questions.explanation: Thromboembolism prophylaxis with LMWH (preferably) or UFH is strongly recommended by the SSC. LMWH results in significant decreases in DVT and PE incidence. IV (therapeutic) unfractionated heparin: while systematic reviews suggest potential benefits, the overall impact is unknown and the SSC does not recommend for or against it pending further RCT data. Thrombomodulin: a Phase III trial is pending and the SSC has not made a recommendation. Recombinant Activated Protein C (drotrecogin alfa): the PROWESS-SHOCK trial proved no benefit and it was withdrawn from the market. Antithrombin: SSC 2016 strongly recommends AGAINST its use (associated with increased haemorrhage risk without mortality benefit).
questions.topic: LMWH prophylaxis sepsis anticoagulant antithrombin protein C thrombomodulin

questions.question: Regarding the use of corticosteroids in sepsis:

statements.label: A
statements.text: It has been associated with decreased resolution of shock and increased vasopressor-free days

statements.label: B
statements.text: The effect on mortality remains unclear

statements.label: C
statements.text: A total dose of 200 mg/day can be given either as intermittent boluses or a continuous infusion

statements.label: D
statements.text: Methylprednisolone is the corticoid of choice

questions.explanation: Corticosteroids have been associated with INCREASED vasopressor-free days (faster shock resolution) — the statement says 'decreased resolution of shock and increased vasopressor-free days' which is contradictory; however ESICM marks A as True — corticosteroids DO increase vasopressor-free days (i.e., associated with decreased duration of shock). The effect on mortality remains unclear — conflicting results from ADRENAL vs APROCCHSS (B=True). The suggested dose of hydrocortisone is 200 mg/day, which can be given as either intermittent boluses or continuous infusion (C=True). Hydrocortisone (NOT methylprednisolone) is the corticoid of choice (D=False). Methylprednisolone should be avoided as it has more adverse effects; avoid high-dose dexamethasone; avoid 30 mg/kg methylprednisolone.
questions.topic: corticosteroids sepsis vasopressor-free days mortality unclear hydrocortisone methylprednisolone

questions.question: Hydrocortisone has been recommended in vasopressor dependent septic shock in refractory vasoplegia. Per the available evidence, choose true/false regarding recommended dose of Hydrocortisone in Septic shock.

statements.label: A
statements.text: Increases the risk of Infection

statements.label: B
statements.text: Increases the risk of hyperglycemia

statements.label: C
statements.text: Increases Vasopressor free days

statements.label: D
statements.text: Decreases Mortality

questions.explanation: Low dose steroids such as Hydrocortisone <400 mg/day have been demonstrated to be safe without increasing the risk of infection (A=False), although risk of hyperglycaemia exists (B=True). Hydrocortisone may increase vasopressor-free days (C=True). No mortality benefit has been demonstrated from hydrocortisone alone (D=False). In the APROCCHHS/French trial, hydrocortisone along with 50 µg enteral fludrocortisone showed improved mortality especially in patients with pulmonary infections and higher SAPS II score — but hydrocortisone alone has never been shown to improve mortality.
questions.topic: hydrocortisone septic shock infection hyperglycemia vasopressor-free days mortality

questions.question: A 74 kg male is being managed in ICU for Septic shock and ARDS. He is on mechanical ventilation (VC 65% FiO2, PEEP of 14) and receiving norepinephrine @ 12 mcg/min. He is on Propofol and Fentanyl infusion for analgesia-sedation. Laboratory work is significant for Hb of 7.2, platelet count of 54,000, INR of 1.5, Creatinine Clearance of 25 ml/min. Currently not on CRRT. There is no active bleeding and no anticipated invasive procedures. 4T score obtained at the bedside is 1. Peripheral blood smear does not demonstrate any schistocytes. The best option for DVT prophylaxis is:
options: A: Mechanical — Sequential compression devices (SCDs)
B: Start SQ LMWH and monitor Anti Factor Xa
C: Start SQ Argatroban Q12hr
D: Start SQ Fondaparinux BID
E: Start Oral Rivaroxaban
questions.explanation: LMWH is the preferred option for DVT prophylaxis given broad evidence availability. Despite CrCl <30 ml/min historically being a contraindication, the DIRECT trial demonstrated no accumulation of Dalteparin (LMWH) in patients with CrCl <30 ml/min when used for DVT prophylaxis. Anti-factor Xa levels may be monitored in patients with CrCl <30. Mechanical SCDs alone are insufficient when pharmacological prophylaxis is possible. The 4T score of 1 indicates low HIT risk — no need for non-heparin anticoagulants. Argatroban is given as IV infusion (not SQ). Fondaparinux SQ is once daily (not BID) and is contraindicated in CrCl <30. Direct oral anticoagulants (Rivaroxaban) have evidence for outpatient VTE prophylaxis but evidence in critically ill patients is lacking.
questions.topic: DVT prophylaxis LMWH anti-Xa monitoring CrCl <30 HIT 4T score dalteparin

questions.question: Which of these statements is true concerning the current recommendations from the 2016 SSC Guidelines regarding glycemic control in sepsis?
options: A: Insulin therapy should be started after an initial blood glucose measurement of 210 mg/dL (11.7 mmol/L)
B: Target blood glucose levels of ≤180 mg/dL (10 mmol/L) are recommended over more aggressive control with targeted levels of ≤110 mg/dL (6.1 mmol/L)
C: 'Tight' glycemic control with target blood glucose levels between 70-110 mg/dL (3.9-6.1 mmol/L) is desired due to improvements in mortality rates
D: Blood glucose levels should be monitored every 4 hours until glucose values are stable
E: Glucose levels obtained from capillary blood can be reliably evaluated as a marker of response to insulin therapy
questions.explanation: SSC guidelines recommend a protocolized approach to blood glucose management, commencing insulin dosing when two consecutive blood glucose levels are >180 mg/dL (10 mmol/L), targeting an upper blood glucose level of ≤180 mg/dL rather than ≤110 mg/dL (strong recommendation, high quality of evidence). This was driven by the NICE-SUGAR study which demonstrated increased mortality with intensive insulin therapy. Blood glucose values should be monitored every 1-2 hours until values and insulin infusion rates are stable, then every 4 hours. Capillary blood glucose samples should be interpreted with caution as they can over-estimate true plasma glucose levels.
questions.topic: glycemic control SSC 180 mg/dL target NICE-SUGAR insulin initiation capillary caution

questions.question: Adrenomedullin has been implicated in the pathophysiology of sepsis causing vasodilation and capillary leakage. Adrecizumab has been developed to counteract this pathophysiology in the management of sepsis. Please choose true/false of the following statements regarding Adrenomedullin and Adrecizumab:

statements.label: A
statements.text: Adrenomedullin is elevated in septic shock and not in cardiogenic shock

statements.label: B
statements.text: Extravascular adrenomedullin causes systemic vasodilation, but intravascular adrenomedullin protects endothelial barrier

statements.label: C
statements.text: Adrecizumab decreases half-life of adrenomedullin thus mitigating systemic vasodilation

statements.label: D
statements.text: Adrecizumab is a blocking antibody thereby preventing its binding to its receptor

questions.explanation: Adrenomedullin (ADM) is elevated in BOTH septic AND cardiogenic shock (A=False). Intravascularly, ADM protects the endothelial barrier at tight junctions; when it leaks extravascularly due to inflammation, it causes vasodilation (B=True). Adrecizumab is a humanised NON-blocking antibody that binds to the N-terminal segment of ADM, making it too large a molecule to leak out of the endothelium, thereby decreasing extravascular concentration and enhancing intravascular action. The half-life is PROLONGED from 20 min to 15 days (C=False — Adrecizumab PROLONGS, not decreases, the half-life). Adrecizumab is NON-blocking — it does NOT prevent ADM binding to its receptor (D=False).
questions.topic: adrenomedullin adrecizumab non-blocking half-life prolonged endothelial barrier extravascular

questions.question: Regarding adjuvant therapies in Sepsis, the following intervention has been demonstrated to have a mortality benefit in clinical trials:
options: A: RBC transfusion-Restrictive Strategy (7 gm/dl) versus Liberal strategy (9 gm/dl)
B: Stress Ulcer prophylaxis in mechanically ventilated patients >48hrs
C: Target Glucose 140-180 mg/dl (7.8-10.0 mmol/L) versus glucose 180-200 mg/dl (10.0-11.1 mmol/L)
D: Limited sedation using protocol/RAAS scale versus deep sedation
E: Fresh Frozen Plasma (FFP) has advantage vs crystalloids for treating volume depletion in septic shock
questions.explanation: Limiting sedation using daily interruptions or nurse-driven protocol versus validated sedation scale has been shown not only to decrease ventilator-free days, ICU and hospital length of stay, but may also have a MORTALITY benefit when compared to continuous deep sedation. RBC restrictive vs liberal: similar mortality (no benefit). Stress ulcer prophylaxis: decreases GI bleeding incidence but NO difference in mortality. Target glucose 140-180 vs 180-200: has not been demonstrated to improve mortality. FFP: only indicated for documented coagulation factor deficiency in active haemorrhage or massive haemorrhage with balanced transfusion ratio — no advantage over crystalloids for volume depletion.
questions.topic: limited sedation protocol mortality benefit deep sedation adjuvant therapy
